name: Zesty Rosemary-Rye Sticks
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon dried rosemary, or 3 tablespoons fresh rosemary, finely chopped
Coarse salt for sprinkling on the baking sheet
recipeInstructions:
"The citrus zest undertone of these versatile bread sticks pairs well with the rosemary and molasses to create an intriguing addition to a picnic basket or a formal dinner. Preheat oven to 325°F.
1. In a small bowl, combine yeast with molasses and warm water. Set aside in a warm place until the yeast starts to foam, about 5 to 10 minutes.
2. Prepare zest by lightly grating the outer peel of orange and lemon against the fine grate of a cheese grater. Use only the colored part of the rind; the underlying white portion is bitter.
3. In a large bowl or food processor, combine flours, salt, and baking soda. Stir in orange zest, lemon zest, and rosemary. Mix well. Add oil, egg, and orange juice; mix until well blended.
4. Add yeast mixture and mix until well blended. Knead dough by hand for about 5 minutes on a lightly floured surface until smooth and springy to the touch. Place dough in a large, lightly oiled bowl and turn over to coat all sides. Cover with a damp towel and set in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 1-1/2 to 2 hours.
5. Punch dough down and turn out onto a lightly floured surface or pastry cloth. Divide into 12 small pieces. Roll each piece into a stick approximately 12 inches long. Place sticks 1 inch apart on a baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal. Cover sheet with a towel and let rise slightly in a warm place for 10 to 15 minutes.
6. Brush tops of the sticks with lightly beaten egg mixture. Sprinkle with coarse salt. If desired, sprinkle chopped rosemary for additional flavor.
7. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, or until well browned. Cool on a rack."
